icons.importPattern: import { RiNameLine } from '@remixicon/react' — use Line (outline) or Fill variants per UI need.
icons.styling: Size with Tailwind (e.g. className="size-4 shrink-0"). Color inherits via currentColor — set text-brandcolor-textstrong, text-brandcolor-textweak, text-brandcolor-primary, etc.
icons.rule: Use Remix Icon for all icons in React/TSX across catalog, admin, and canvas. Do not mix other icon sets (Heroicons, Lucide, Material Icons) for chrome unless a one-off exception is documented. See tailwind.config.js header comment for the same rule.
icons.canvasHtmlFragmentWebfont: For model-generated HTML (canvas htmlSnippet blocks), icons may use `<i class="ri-name-line">` or `ri-name-fill` per https://remixicon.com — index.html loads remixicon.css so these classes render. Combine with Tailwind (e.g. inline-flex size-5 text-brandcolor-textstrong). React/TSX elsewhere still uses @remixicon/react imports.

componentGuidelines.canvasStylingContract: Components canvas: Tailwind utilities from tailwind.config.js only (no arbitrary hex in canvas JSX). Icons: Remix Icon (@remixicon/react) per theme-guide icons.rule. Neutral publish classes live in canvas-node-publish.ts + index.css. Confirm-password valid ring: theme.extend.boxShadow.confirm-password-valid + overflow-visible shell on that block.

button.primaryCanvasAlias: primary-canvas-button — same paint as canvas-primary-button (use for in-canvas primary CTAs when needed).
button.primaryDescription: Primary CTA: canvas-primary-button or primary-canvas-button in src/index.css (theme() brandcolor-primary / brandcolor-primaryhover). Label text-brandcolor-white. Do not use brandcolor-badge-success-* for primary or for canvas Published state.
button.primaryEquivalentTailwind: inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-brandcolor-white bg-brandcolor-primary hover:bg-brandcolor-primaryhover active:shadow-button-press focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 appearance-none border-0
button.secondaryCanvasAlias: secondary-canvas-button — same paint as canvas-secondary-button (in-canvas secondary CTAs).
button.secondaryDescription: Secondary CTA: canvas-secondary-button or secondary-canvas-button in src/index.css (theme() brandcolor-secondary / brandcolor-secondaryhover). Label text-brandcolor-white.
button.secondaryEquivalentTailwind: inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-brandcolor-white bg-brandcolor-secondary hover:bg-brandcolor-secondaryhover active:shadow-button-press focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 appearance-none border-0
button.componentsCanvasExample: ComponentsCanvasSurface: world pan/zoom; initial card + primary + secondary + neutral + confirm password + text field; localStorage agentic-components-canvas-nodes-v1. Kinds: card, primaryButton, secondaryButton, neutralButton, confirmPasswordInput, textInputField, productSidebar (CanvasPlan v2). Catalog ids canvas-card-* / canvas-primary-* / canvas-secondary-* / canvas-neutral-* / canvas-confirm-password-* / canvas-text-field-* / canvas-product-sidebar-*. Prune: scripts/prune-canvas-catalog.mjs.
button.neutral: Reference — neutral canvas: index.css .neutral-canvas-button; strokeweak border, hover strokelight; publish string in canvas-node-publish.ts
button.buttonBrandGlowShadow: Optional primary-tinted elevation for buttons or focus: theme.extend.boxShadow.button-brand-glow → Tailwind utility shadow-button-brand-glow. Three layered oklch(0.5243 0.1143 214.28 / alpha) glows (stronger spread) aligned with brandcolor-primary (#F84416). Compose with active:shadow-button-press or hover as needed.

componentsCanvasCard.description: Default block on ComponentsCanvasSurface — same surface token as componentGuidelines.card; typography uses tailwind.config.js brand text colors only. Hover help: parent group/canvas-tip + .canvas-element-tooltip (+ optional .canvas-element-tooltip-detail); avoid title= on canvas chrome to prevent OS tooltips.
componentsCanvasCard.title: font-sans text-base font-semibold text-brandcolor-textstrong
componentsCanvasCard.subtitle: mt-1 text-sm text-brandcolor-textweak

componentsCanvasPrimaryButton.description: Components canvas block kind primaryButton: wrapped in same card surface as componentsCanvasCard; inner control uses primary-canvas-button (src/index.css) so fill is theme brandcolor-primary and hover brandcolor-primaryhover (tailwind.config.js). Published sourceHtml uses the same Tailwind utility names as componentGuidelines.button.primaryEquivalentTailwind for static export. Catalog componentId prefix canvas-primary-.

componentsCanvasSecondaryButton.description: Components canvas block kind secondaryButton: same outer chrome as componentsCanvasCard; inner control uses secondary-canvas-button (src/index.css) for brandcolor-secondary and hover brandcolor-secondaryhover (tailwind.config.js). Published sourceHtml uses componentGuidelines.button.secondaryEquivalentTailwind. Catalog componentId prefix canvas-secondary-.

componentsCanvasNeutralButton.description: Canvas neutralButton: same card chrome; neutral-canvas-button in index.css. Catalog prefix canvas-neutral-.

componentsCanvasConfirmPasswordInput.description: Canvas confirmPasswordInput: .confirm-password-canvas-input — default border-brandcolor-strokeweak; :focus and :user-valid use border (1px) border-brandcolor-primary + theme boxShadow.confirm-password-valid (0 0 0 2px #F8441633). Shell clipShell=false. Catalog prefix canvas-confirm-password-.

componentsCanvasProductSidebar.description: Generative productSidebar (CanvasPlan version 2): nav shell aligned with components canvas contract — Tailwind tokens only, Remix icons via fixed iconKey maps in code (no free-form icon names from the model). Preview width 260px; world chrome uses CanvasWorldBlock chromeVariant sidebar: only border-r border-brandcolor-strokeweak on the outer shell (no full box border). Inner surface bg-brandcolor-white; header border-b border-brandcolor-strokeweak; section headings text-[11px] uppercase text-brandcolor-textweak; rows hover:bg-brandcolor-fill. Optional search uses text-field-canvas-input; optional CTA uses neutral-canvas-button. Published sourceHtml: buildProductSidebarPublishHtml in canvas-node-publish.ts (nav icons omitted in static HTML). Catalog prefix canvas-product-sidebar-.
componentsCanvasProductSidebar.borderRule: Outer preview: border-0 border-r border-brandcolor-strokeweak only (see CanvasWorldBlock chromeVariant sidebar).
componentsCanvasProductSidebar.iconKeysNav: home | folder | task | fileText | key | history | none

caseCard.description: Case study card: case name (h2) with share + tag icon actions on the header row, subheading (text-strong), description (text-weak body).
caseCard.surface: Same as card — rounded-lg border border-brandcolor-strokeweak bg-brandcolor-white shadow-card

profileCard.description: Profile / identity card: name row with trailing icon actions, title line, two body lines. Uses same surface as card.
profileCard.name: componentGuidelines.heading.h2 — font-sans text-xl font-semibold text-brandcolor-textstrong
profileCard.title: text-sm text-brandcolor-textweak (role / subtitle under name)
profileCard.iconButton: h-8 w-8 rounded-md border border-transparent text-brandcolor-textstrong hover:bg-brandcolor-fill active:border active:border-brandcolor-strokeweak focus:outline-none focus-visible:border focus-visible:border-brandcolor-strokeweak

heading.h1: font-sans text-3xl font-semibold tracking-tight text-brandcolor-textstrong c_md:text-4xl

componentGuidelines.input: w-full rounded-md border border-brandcolor-strokestrong bg-brandcolor-white px-3 py-2 text-sm text-brandcolor-textstrong placeholder:text-brandcolor-textweak focus:border-brandcolor-textstrong focus:outline-none focus:ring-0

themeConfigurationTypography.description: Catalog Theme > Typography (src/pages/catalog/theme): text-style rows and monospace fields use brand tokens from tailwind.config.js theme.extend.colors.
themeConfigurationTypography.compactRows: Full-width row buttons: default bg-brandcolor-fill, hover:bg-brandcolor-strokelight, cursor-pointer (ThemeTypographyFsLhCompactRow).
themeConfigurationTypography.textFields: Border border-brandcolor-strokeweak bg-brandcolor-white; hover:bg-brandcolor-fill; focus (active) border-brandcolor-primary — font stack inputs, font-weight inputs, and modal fs/lh wrappers (focus-within:border-brandcolor-primary on wrapper).

themeConfigurationShadows.description: Catalog Theme > Shadows (ThemeShadowsPanel): same compact row pattern as typography text styles; full value edited in TypographySettingModal (titleId shadow-setting-modal-title) with textarea inside strokeweak border, hover:bg-brandcolor-fill, focus-within:border-brandcolor-primary wrapper.
themeConfigurationShadows.compactRows: Per-token row: bg-brandcolor-fill, hover:bg-brandcolor-strokelight, shows key + truncated value (ThemeShadowsPanel).

themeConfigurationSpacing.description: Catalog Theme > Spacing (ThemeSpacingPanel, /catalog/theme/spacing). Section order matches the page: panelIntro → cardSemanticAliases → howSpacingWorks → quickReferenceIntro → table (live) → editTokensIntro. Primitives: micro…inline; card aliases: card-pad-* / card-gap-* (see theme-card-spacing-semantics.ts). Detail for models: use panelIntro, cardSemanticAliases, howSpacingWorks, quickReferenceIntro, editTokensIntro below.
themeConfigurationSpacing.panelIntro: Values map to --space-* on :root and Tailwind theme.extend.spacing (e.g. p-cozy, gap-tight). Only rem or px lengths. The quick reference table on the page reflects the current theme values from the editor.

cardSemanticAliases.readOnlyNote: Read-only: --card-padding-* and --card-gap-* on :root point at --space-* primitives. Implementation map: src/config/theme-card-spacing-semantics.ts. Use Tailwind p-card-pad-* and gap-card-gap-* when prompts describe card shell density or inner card gaps; editing a primitive token updates every alias that references it. Full prompt phrase lists: spacing.cardPaddingSemantics.promptVocabulary and spacing.cardGapSemantics.promptVocabulary.

paragraphs: Think inside → out: padding is space between a component's border and its content; margin is space outside the border that pushes neighbors away. Gap is space between siblings in a flex or grid—like margin, but owned by the parent stack.

Names that feel closer in language (micro, tight) are meant to read as closer on screen than cozy or section. Use smaller tokens inside a single component (icon + label, title + meta), cozy for the card shell, and section or hero for breathing room between blocks on the page.
howSpacingWorks.approxPxNote: Approximate pixel hints assume a 16px root font size for rem; layout still follows real rem and user settings.
howSpacingWorks.quickReferenceAnchorText: On the Spacing page, the quick reference block (id spacing-quick-ref) shows live decode per token.

themeConfigurationSpacing.quickReferenceIntro: Columns: Token, Value, ~Px (documentary @ 16px root for rem), Tailwind examples, When to use. Row click opens edit (TypographySettingModal, spacing-setting-modal-title); trailing info icon opens per-token help (spacing-help-modal-title) without edit.
themeConfigurationSpacing.editTokensIntro: Click a row to change the length. Use the info icon for prompt phrases and layout guidance without entering edit mode.
themeConfigurationSpacing.insideOutSummary: Inside a component: micro/tight for dense inner rhythm (icon+label, title+meta). Card or panel shell: cozy padding. Between major blocks on the page: section or hero. Horizontal rows (chips, buttons): inline.
themeConfigurationSpacing.approxPxAssumption: UI and theme-guide ~px hints assume a 16px root for rem→px conversion only; real layout follows rem and user font settings.
promptVocabulary: micro gap / hairline — maps to micro
cozy padding / comfortable card inner — cozy
hero spacing / large vertical break — hero
themeConfigurationSpacing.figmaScaleBridge: Rough mapping for handoff language (not new tokens): micro ≈ XXS/4px inner; tight ≈ XS stacks inside a frame; cozy ≈ S card padding; section ≈ M–L between frames; hero ≈ XL page rhythm; inline ≈ horizontal auto-layout gap between row siblings.
themeConfigurationSpacing.tokens: micro (hairline), tight (dense), cozy (default card padding / stack), section (larger break), hero (largest vertical rhythm), inline (horizontal row / chip gaps).
themeConfigurationSpacing.examplePrompt: Create a card with white background, stroke-weak border, rounded corners and p-cozy. Title text-strong; stack actions with gap-tight.
themeConfigurationSpacing.compactRows: Full-width row opens edit (TypographySettingModal, spacing-setting-modal-title); trailing info icon opens per-token help without edit. Same monospace length field and padding preview as before; valid values show a Decode: ~px line below the textarea.

themeConfigurationColors.description: Catalog Theme > Colors (ThemeColorsPanel): tokens grouped in brand-color-theme-groups.ts (text, stroke, brand, surfaces, banners, archived, status, badges). Each row: mono token + Reset; swatch + hex field with same hover/focus as typography inputs.
themeConfigurationColors.groupsSource: src/pages/catalog/theme/brand-color-theme-groups.ts — must list every BRAND_COLOR_KEYS entry once.
themeConfigurationColors.tokenHelp: Optional per-token naming stories: TOKEN_COLOR_HELP in src/pages/catalog/theme/token-color-help.tsx — document icon after hex opens TypographySettingModal (text-strong and text-weak first; add keys to scale).

spacing.description: 4-point grid: use multiples of 4px for margins, padding, and gaps. Tailwind spacing utilities map 1:1 (space-1 = 0.25rem = 4px at 16px root). Layout plans use semantic afterGap tokens (aliases below). Prefer semantic theme spacing tokens (spacing.tokenReference) in prompts and JSX. For card shells, prefer spacing.cardPaddingSemantics / cardGapSemantics utilities when the user describes density in natural language.

tight.useWhen: Related lines inside a component (title + meta)

cozy.useWhen: Default card / panel inner padding and stacks

section.useWhen: Between major groups inside a page or form

hero.useWhen: Strong break before first content or between sections

inline.useWhen: Horizontal rows: chips, buttons, label + control

cardPaddingSemantics.description: Card shell padding aliases on :root (--card-padding-*) reference var(--space-*) only; see src/config/theme-card-spacing-semantics.ts. Editing primitive tokens in Theme > Spacing updates resolved card padding with no duplicate lengths.

primitiveMap.compact: inline — dense card, dashboard widget, tight inner shell
primitiveMap.comfy: section — spacious, promo, or onboarding-style card

promptVocabulary: compact card padding / dense card / small info card → p-card-pad-compact
default card padding / normal card / standard card → p-card-pad-default
comfy card padding / spacious card / highlight card → p-card-pad-comfy

cardGapSemantics.description: Vertical or flex gaps inside a card (--card-gap-* alias to --space-*). Same single-source rule as card padding.
cardGapSemantics.tailwind: gap-card-gap-tight, gap-card-gap-default, gap-card-gap-loose (e.g. flex flex-col gap-card-gap-default).

primitiveMap.loose: cozy — roomier stack inside a large card

promptVocabulary: tight gaps inside the card / dense stack → gap-card-gap-tight
loose inner card gaps / roomy stack → gap-card-gap-loose

spaceScaleUsage.space-1: 4px — icon to text, micro gaps (Tailwind gap-1 / mt-1).
spaceScaleUsage.space-4: 16px — default stack gap between fields (gap-4 / mb-4).

scale.tight: mb-2 or gap-2 — 8px (space-2); title–subtitle stack inside pageHeading chrome uses space-y-1 (4px) in UI.
scale.default: mb-4 or gap-4 — 16px (space-4); between related catalog blocks in a form stack.
scale.section: mb-6 or gap-6 — 24px (space-6); between major sections or before a new chrome heading after catalog.
scale.hero: mb-8 or gap-8 — 32px (space-8); strong break after a hero title before first component.

themeSpacingTailwind.micro: p-micro, m-micro, gap-micro — 4px scale step; icon to label.
themeSpacingTailwind.cozy: p-cozy, px-cozy, gap-cozy — 16px default card inner padding and related vertical gaps.
themeSpacingTailwind.section: mt-section, gap-section, py-section — 24px between major blocks.
themeSpacingTailwind.inline: gap-inline, px-inline — 12px horizontal rhythm in rows.

semantics: Title and subtitle inside one chrome block: tight rhythm (space-y-1 ≈ space-1 between lines).
afterGap on a block sets space BEFORE the next block (margin below this block). Last block has no bottom margin.
Structured preview: chrome→catalog and catalog→catalog infer default (space-4) when afterGap is omitted; catalog→chrome uses section (space-6).
Catalog sourceHtml roots: avoid h-full justify-center and heavy outer p-*; use w-full flex-col justify-start so the composer controls vertical rhythm.
Repeat instances in one catalog block (flow): vertical stack with gap-4 (space-4) between cells.

Layout plan row: 2–4 columns of chrome/catalog leaves. Default row is side-by-side from 640px (stack only on very narrow viewports). stackBelow md|lg = stack until that breakpoint. split: sidebarMain; sidebarWidth narrow|default|wide.
Nested row/split inside columns is not allowed — only chrome and catalog leaves inside row columns and split regions.

criticalRules: Do not use raw hex color values in JSX, CSS modules, or inline styles for branded UI.

Icons: use Remix Icon for React only (@remixicon/react). Import named components from https://remixicon.com; style with Tailwind size-* and text-brandcolor-* — see theme-guide icons block and tailwind.config.js header.
Do not use default Tailwind palette utilities (e.g. bg-gray-*, text-blue-*) for branded surfaces; use bg-brandcolor-*, text-brandcolor-*, border-brandcolor-* only.
Buttons use rounded-md (6px) per theme; cards use rounded-lg with border-brandcolor-strokeweak and shadow-card.

Layout plan: if the user wants two fields side by side or on one row, output ONE type row with two columns (one catalog leaf per column)—never two top-level catalog blocks. Use type split for sidebar + main. afterGap / defaultAfterGap: tight, default, section, hero. Client infers afterGap: row/split next to catalog/chrome uses default (space-4) like stacked fields; section between row↔split or catalog→chrome unless overridden.
Do not use arbitrary mt-48, mb-20, or one-off pixel classes for stack gaps; refer to spacing.spaceScaleRem and layoutTokenMap.

Prefer theme spacing tokens from spacing.themeSpacingTailwind for padding/margin/gap: micro, tight, cozy, section, hero, inline (e.g. p-cozy, gap-tight). For card shells described in natural language (compact vs default vs comfy card padding), prefer spacing.cardPaddingSemantics / cardGapSemantics utilities (p-card-pad-default, gap-card-gap-tight, etc.); primitives remain valid when the user or spec names them explicitly.
Catalog blueprint roots: no h-full justify-center on layout previews; minimal outer padding so gaps match spacing.semantics.
